blocks.body: A statement that two ratios are equal: a/b = c/d. Example: 1/2 = 3/6 is a proportion because both equal 0.5.
Cross-Multiplication: If a/b = c/d, then a x d = b x c. This is the key tool for solving proportions. Cross multiply to eliminate fractions, then solve for the unknown.

blocks.label: Example 1
blocks.body: Solve: x/3 = 4/6. Cross multiply: 6x = 12. Divide by 6: x = 2.

blocks.label: Example 2
blocks.body: Solve: 5/8 = 15/x. Cross multiply: 5x = 120. Divide by 5: x = 24.

blocks.label: Example 3
blocks.body: If 4 apples cost $1.20, how much do 10 apples cost? Set up: 4/1.20 = 10/x. Cross multiply: 4x = 12. x = $3.00.

blocks.label: Example 4
blocks.body: Map scale: 1 inch = 50 miles. Two cities are 3.5 inches apart. Distance = ? Set up: 1/50 = 3.5/x -> x = 175 miles.
Always label your proportion with units. Make sure you have the same type of quantity in the same position on both sides.
